title: driftdockerprofiler Event
description: One JSONL line written by driftdockerprofiler.writer.JsonlWriter to /trace/events.log. Every event is exactly one of the four variants below, discriminated by the `type` field. The combined `$defs` are the source of truth — observability-server's openapi.yaml mirrors this shape under components.schemas.

Frame.description: One stack frame, leaf-first. Mirrors pprof's Location+Line+Function triple, flattened for JSON. Phase F1a: the optional `language`, `is_native`, and `is_system` fields mirror `drift-static-profiler/schema/profile.schema.json#/$defs/Frame` so dynamic and static profiles can be joined on `(file, name)` and rendered through one viewer.

qualified_name.description: Phase F1b. Fully-qualified function name, e.g. `OrderService.create`. Sourced from CPython's `co_qualname` (Python 3.11+); absent on 3.7-3.10. Lets a viewer join this Frame to the static profiler's Frame on `(file, qualified_name)` — a stronger key than `(file, name)` because it disambiguates same-named methods in different classes. Mirrors static schema's Frame.qualified_name.

module.description: Phase F1b. Containing module / package name, e.g. `orders.service`. Sourced from the frame's `f_globals['__name__']`. Mirrors static schema's Frame.module.

language.description: Source language for this frame. Always 'python' for frames produced by this profiler. Mirrors static schema's Frame.language.

is_native.description: Whether this is a native (C/C++/etc.) frame. Always false — this profiler emits Python-level frames; the C++ SIGPROF extension also returns Python frames via the interpreter API. Mirrors static schema's Frame.is_native.

is_system.description: True iff the agent-skip filter (BUILTIN_EXCLUDE_PATHS + STRICT_USER_CODE_EXCLUDE_PATHS, see client.py) would classify this frame as stdlib / runtime / profiler-self. Same source of truth as `_should_exclude`, just exposed as a per-frame label. Mirrors static schema's Frame.is_system.

Sample.description: One unique call stack with its tick count. Only appears inside bundle events (samples[]).

value_ns.description: count * period_ns — total time attributed to this stack.

labels.description: Optional key/value labels (reserved; currently always empty).

EventBase.description: Fields shared by every event variant. Realised via allOf in each variant so the on-disk JSON is flat.

type.description: Discriminator. Use this to pick the matching $defs entry.

time.description: ISO-8601 UTC, microsecond precision, ending in 'Z'. Window-close timestamp.

cpu.description: 1-min system load average at window close (os.getloadavg()[0]).

memory_bytes.description: Process RSS at window close (Linux: /proc/self/statm; macOS: getrusage().ru_maxrss).

memory_peak_bytes.description: Peak RSS observed since process start (getrusage().ru_maxrss). Monotonic non-decreasing. Useful for spotting transient spikes that the current-RSS probe may have missed.

WallTraceEvent.title: WallTraceEvent
WallTraceEvent.description: Per-trace mode, wall-clock sampler. One event per unique call stack per window. Wall ticks fire every period_ns whether the process is on-CPU or sleeping.

CPUTraceEvent.title: CPUTraceEvent
CPUTraceEvent.description: Per-trace mode, CPU sampler (C++ extension, SIGPROF). One event per unique call stack per window. CPU ticks fire only when the process is on-CPU — idle time is not counted.

WallProfileEvent.title: WallProfileEvent
WallProfileEvent.description: Bundle mode, wall-clock sampler. One event per window with every unique stack inlined under samples[]. Closer to pprof's model than per-trace mode.

CPUProfileEvent.title: CPUProfileEvent
CPUProfileEvent.description: Bundle mode, CPU sampler (C++ extension). One event per window with every unique stack inlined under samples[].

FunctionCallEvent.title: FunctionCallEvent
FunctionCallEvent.description: Deterministic-tracer event. One emitted per call of a function decorated with `@driftdockerprofiler.trace`. Catches every invocation regardless of duration — useful for methods too fast to be sampled by `wall_trace`/`cpu_trace` (anything below ~one sample period). The two layers coexist in the same JSONL file; consumers filter by `type`.

duration_ns.description: Wall-time the call took, in nanoseconds.

status.description: `error` if the function raised any exception.

ProfileMetadataEvent.title: ProfileMetadataEvent
ProfileMetadataEvent.description: Phase F2. Session-header event emitted ONCE per `Client.start()`, before any sample event. Mirrors `drift-static-profiler/schema/profile.schema.json` top-level fields (`schema_version`, `mode`, `generator`) so a consumer reading the JSONL stream linearly gets the same metadata block a static profile carries — and a converter can emit an exact `profile.schema.json` document with `mode: "sampled"` from this header plus the sample events that follow.

time.description: ISO-8601 UTC, microsecond precision, ending in 'Z'. Session-start instant.

mode.description: Mirrors `profile.schema.json::mode`. Always `sampled` for events produced by this runtime profiler; the static profiler emits `static`.

schema_version.description: Mirrors `profile.schema.json::schema_version`. Bumped when the dynamic-side wire format changes.

service_id.description: Per-session UUID (hex, no dashes). Stays stable for every event in this JSONL stream; lets a deployment-wide consumer that aggregates across many pods dedup samples from the same Python process restart.

generator.description: Provenance block. Field shapes are 1:1 copies of `profile.schema.json::Generator` so the F4 converter is a trivial field copy.

captured_at.description: ISO timestamp of session start. Same instant as `time` on the envelope; exists so the Generator block is self-contained.

source_root.description: `os.getcwd()` at session start. Same convention the static profiler uses, so a viewer can join on this key without translation.

ProfileBundleFields.description: Bundle-only fields shared by WallProfileEvent + CPUProfileEvent.

time_ns.description: Window-close timestamp as ns int. Same instant as `time` (ISO) — pprof carryover.

sample_type.description: pprof-style value-type vector. [0] is always ('sample','count'); [1] is the profile-specific value type — ('wall','nanoseconds') or ('cpu','nanoseconds').

samples.description: All unique stacks seen in the window.
